author: Corpus Analyst Rowan-C

dimensionSummaries.dimension.player-fantasy: The player is a sea-roaming rescuer and practical navigator who turns sketches, charts, and a stylus-controlled hero into progress across an unfamiliar archipelago.
dimensionSummaries.dimension.core-loop: The recurring cadence alternates plotting a voyage, landing on an island, solving a bounded objective, gaining a chart or capability, and returning to the sea with a wider choice of destinations.
dimensionSummaries.dimension.verbs: Touching directs movement and inspection, drawn strokes produce attacks or paths, and acquired tools extend the vocabulary with ranged, digging, grappling, and context-sensitive actions.
dimensionSummaries.dimension.world-state: Recovered sea charts expose new sectors, dungeon results unlock routes and tools, collected sand extends protected time, and ship equipment changes voyage resilience.
dimensionSummaries.dimension.topology: The world is a node-and-route archipelago whose islands contain compact local graphs, while a repeatedly entered central temple acts as a deepening progression spine.
dimensionSummaries.dimension.gating-revisit: Sea sectors, island obstacles, and deeper temple floors are gated by charts, tools, protected-time capacity, and learned shortcuts that make earlier spaces cheaper to traverse.
dimensionSummaries.dimension.puzzle-information: The game distributes clues across scenery, dialogue, the upper display, and maps that accept player notes, allowing remembered symbols and spatial relationships to become manipulable information.
dimensionSummaries.dimension.dungeon-structure: Island dungeons provide distinct tool-and-boss arcs, while the recurring timed temple layers stealth, safe areas, keys, shortcuts, and newly reachable depths into a cumulative challenge.
dimensionSummaries.dimension.exploration-reward: Optional islands, salvaged treasure, hidden chests, ship parts, health growth, and additional sand convert curiosity into either immediate supplies or longer future expeditions.
dimensionSummaries.dimension.growth-economy: Rupees, consumables, treasure, health, hourglass sand, and interchangeable ship parts create linked personal and vessel economies rather than a single linear power track.
dimensionSummaries.dimension.combat-boss: Stylus attacks emphasize target position and stroke direction, while enemies and bosses ask the player to combine direct movement with a recently learned tool or arena rule.
dimensionSummaries.dimension.npc-quest: Ciela, Linebeck, island residents, and service characters supply motives, transport, clues, trades, and optional errands that repeatedly redirect the voyage graph.
dimensionSummaries.dimension.narrative-performance: The search for the missing Tetra is staged through departures, arrivals, companion exchanges, repeated temple descents, and boss resolutions that return agency quickly to navigation.
dimensionSummaries.dimension.camera-blocking: A mostly overhead play view keeps touch targets and room causality readable, while the second screen can carry map or status information without covering the active scene.
dimensionSummaries.dimension.teaching-feedback: Direct cursor-to-avatar response, visible route lines, map marks, safe-zone color, a draining time meter, and item-specific audiovisual cues expose the result of each new rule.
dimensionSummaries.dimension.ui-input: The stylus is the primary pointer for travel, combat, interaction, item use, route drawing, and annotation, with the two displays separating action from reference information.
dimensionSummaries.dimension.recovery-accessibility: Safe areas pause the central temple's time pressure, opened shortcuts reduce repeated work, and leaving to gather more sand or equipment provides a recoverable alternative to brute-force repetition.
dimensionSummaries.dimension.perspective-implementation: The 2.5D overhead view maps touch coordinates directly to ground positions and preserves readable silhouettes, while sea travel shifts scale without abandoning map-led navigation.
dimensionSummaries.dimension.hardware-multiplayer: The original uses the DS touch screen, dual displays, microphone, local wireless, download play, and online services; its separate two-player mode turns drawn paths into asymmetric pursuit control.
dimensionSummaries.dimension.production-runtime: Reproduction requires gesture and path recognition, persistent handwritten map layers, dual-screen layouts, boat navigation, timed protected zones, companion state, and deterministic dungeon shortcuts.

observationThemes.context: During ordinary island traversal, the touch screen presents Link, nearby terrain, enemies, and interactive targets in one pointer-controlled field.
preconditions: The player is in a controllable field or dungeon area
A destination, target, or nearby enemy is visible
playerInput: Touch a ground position to move
Trace or tap near a target to attack or interact
before: Link is stationary or following an earlier destination
The target has not been reached or affected
action: Use the stylus position and stroke as the command
after: Link follows the indicated path or performs the contextual action
The target changes state if the command is valid
feedback: Avatar motion follows the pointer destination
Attack motion, hit response, or interaction presentation confirms recognition
consequences: Navigation, combat, and inspection share one learnable physical vocabulary
The same path-drawing hardware can support a separate competitive control rule
observationThemes.factualDescription: The original game uses stylus touches and strokes as the primary means to move Link, inspect the scene, and perform combat actions on the active touch display.
observationThemes.interpretation: A single spatial input language reduces mode switching and makes the player feel like a navigator issuing direct marks on the adventure surface.

observationThemes.context: After securing a new sea chart or equivalent voyage information, previously unknown waters and island destinations become available from the ship.
preconditions: The player has completed the required local objective
A new chart sector has been obtained
playerInput: Draw a navigable route toward a newly shown destination
before: The destination is absent from the usable voyage graph
The current set of islands limits further objectives
action: Register the chart and commit a route across the newly exposed sector
after: Additional sea nodes and routes are usable
A new island objective can be entered
feedback: The chart visibly gains geography and destinations
The ship follows the plotted line while the new sector is presented at travel scale
consequences: The explore-island-return cadence begins another cycle
Optional detours in the expanded sector can yield treasure and vessel improvements
observationThemes.factualDescription: Progress awards sea-chart information that reveals additional waters; the player then draws a ship route and can reach newly available islands and optional discoveries.
observationThemes.interpretation: Making world expansion both a visible map-state change and a player-authored route turns narrative progress into an immediately legible increase in navigational agency.

observationThemes.context: The recurring central temple drains protected time outside designated safe areas and is revisited after the player gains more sand, tools, or shortcuts.
preconditions: The hourglass contains a finite amount of protective sand
playerInput: Leave a safe area and traverse solved or newly reachable rooms
Use an acquired shortcut or tool
Return to safety before the resource is exhausted
before: Deeper floors are blocked by capability, route cost, or insufficient protected time
Previously cleared rooms still lie on the descent
action: Spend protected time while applying accumulated knowledge and capabilities
after: A deeper checkpoint, chart, or shortcut is reached
Later visits can bypass part of the repeated route
feedback: The hourglass meter visibly drains or pauses
Safe flooring and opened shortcuts clearly change traversal pressure
consequences: Earlier mastery becomes measurable time saved on later descents
A failed or inefficient attempt can be answered by leaving, improving capacity, and retrying
observationThemes.factualDescription: The central temple is revisited under a finite protection timer; safe zones halt the drain, while later tools, additional sand, and shortcuts permit deeper and faster traversal.
observationThemes.interpretation: A repeated dungeon can feel progressive when persistent shortcuts and player fluency convert old rooms from repetition into an efficiency test with recoverable preparation options.

observationThemes.context: A clue encountered in one location must be retained or compared with spatial information shown on the map or second display.
preconditions: A relevant symbol, route, order, or landmark has been observed
playerInput: Write a note, symbol, or route on it
Apply the recorded information at the later obstacle
before: The clue and its application are separated by time or space
The correct relationship is not yet externalized
action: Convert observed information into a persistent player-authored map mark
after: The clue remains visible for comparison
The later puzzle can be acted on without relying only on memory
feedback: The handwritten mark remains over the map
The active overhead scene visibly responds when the recorded rule is applied
consequences: Information gathering becomes an explicit player verb
The dual-screen overhead presentation supports reference without obscuring the causal scene
observationThemes.factualDescription: The map can retain freehand stylus notes, allowing the player to record observed spatial or symbolic information and consult it while solving later obstacles.
observationThemes.interpretation: Treating note-taking as part of the interface lets puzzles test observation and translation rather than undocumented recall, while preserving player-authored reasoning.

observationThemes.context: At the end of an island or temple objective, a boss encounter tests the area's tool rule before the companions resume the voyage.
preconditions: The local dungeon route is complete
The relevant item or interaction rule has been learned
playerInput: Use movement, stylus attacks, and the relevant tool
before: The local story objective remains unresolved
The next voyage sector or resource gain is unavailable
action: Create and exploit the boss vulnerability until the encounter resolves
after: The boss is defeated and the local objective closes
Health, sand, treasure, or voyage information advances the campaign
feedback: Arena reactions and damage states confirm the effective tactic
Companion dialogue and reward presentation mark the return from combat to travel
consequences: The player is better prepared for the repeated temple and sea hazards
The rescue journey receives another performed beat rather than only a textual update
observationThemes.factualDescription: Major local objectives culminate in bosses that use the area's learned interaction rule; victory grants progression and returns Link and his companions to the wider voyage.
observationThemes.interpretation: Closing each island arc with a rule exam and a concrete travel benefit binds combat mastery, companion motivation, and the navigation economy into one cadence.

contributionHypotheses: Player-authored route lines and notes can make a map an action surface rather than a passive record.
A repeatedly visited challenge space can show growth through saved time, shortcuts, and increased safe-resource capacity.
Alternating compact island objectives with plotted travel can keep a broad world legible as a node-and-route adventure.
uniqueOrNonportable: The named hourglass, central temple identity, exact floor sequence, character identities, archipelago layout, ship designs, dialogue, and audiovisual motifs are nonportable surface expression.
Specific glyphs, touch-screen puzzle answers, boss solutions, chart geography, and handwritten clue content remain evidence only; only the abstract information transaction may transfer.
openQuestions: A later variant review should separate original online-service behavior from currently unavailable Nintendo Wi-Fi functionality and emulated rereleases.
